components.name: accordion
components.title: Angular Accordion Component
components.description: Accordion groups a collection of contents in tabs.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader Accordion header elements have a button role and use aria-controls to define the id of the content section along with aria-expanded for the visibility state. The value to read a header element defaults to the value of the header property and can be customized by defining an aria-label or aria-labelledby property. Each header has a heading role, for which the level is customized by headerAriaLevel and has a default level of 2 as per W3C specifications. Disabled accordions headers use aria-disabled and are excluded from the keybord navigation. The content uses region role, defines an id that matches the aria-controls of the header and aria-labelledby referring to the id of the header. Header Keyboard Support

sections.label: Basic
sections.description: Accordion is defined using AccordionPanel , AccordionHeader and AccordionContent components. Each AccordionPanel must contain a unique value property to specify the active item.

components.name: autocomplete
components.title: Angular AutoComplete Component
components.description: AutoComplete is an input component that provides real-time suggestions when being typed.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader Value to describe the component can either be provided via label tag combined with inputId prop or using ariaLabelledBy , ariaLabel props. The input element has combobox role in addition to aria-autocomplete , aria-haspopup and aria-expanded attributes. The relation between the input and the popup is created with aria-controls and aria-activedescendant attribute is used to instruct screen reader which option to read during keyboard navigation within the popup list. In multiple mode, chip list uses listbox role whereas each chip has the option role with aria-label set to the label of the chip. The popup list has an id that refers to the aria-controls attribute of the input element and uses listbox as the role. Each list item has option role and an id to match the aria-activedescendant of the input element.

sections.label: advanced-chips-doc
sections.description: This example demonstrates an advanced use case with templating, object handling, dropdown, and multiple mode.

sections.label: basic-chips-doc
sections.description: With ⁠multiple enabled, the AutoComplete component behaves like a chips or tags input. Use addOnBlur , ⁠addOnTab , and ⁠separator properties to customize the keystroke behavior for adding items.

sections.label: Basic
sections.description: AutoComplete uses ngModel for two-way binding, requires a list of suggestions and a completeMethod to query for the results. The completeMethod gets the query text as event.query property and should update the suggestions with the search results.

sections.label: clear-icon-doc
sections.description: When showClear is enabled, a clear icon is displayed to clear the value.

sections.label: Disabled
sections.description: When disabled is present, the element cannot be edited and focused.

sections.label: Dropdown
sections.description: Enabling dropdown property displays a button next to the input field where click behavior of the button is defined using dropdownMode property that takes blank or current as possible values. blank is the default mode to send a query with an empty string whereas current setting sends a query with the current value of the input.

sections.label: Filled
sections.description: Specify the variant property as filled to display the component with a higher visual emphasis than the default outlined style.

sections.label: float-label-doc
sections.description: A floating label appears on top of the input field when focused. Visit FloatLabel documentation for more information.

sections.label: Fluid
sections.description: The fluid prop makes the component take up the full width of its container when set to true.

sections.label: force-selection-doc
sections.description: ForceSelection mode validates the manual input to check whether it also exists in the suggestions list, if not the input value is cleared to make sure the value passed to the model is always one of the suggestions.

sections.label: Group
sections.description: Option grouping is enabled when group property is set to true . group template is available to customize the option groups. All templates get the option instance as the default local template variable.

sections.label: Invalid
sections.description: The invalid state is applied using the ⁠invalid property to indicate failed validation, which can be integrated with Angular Forms.

sections.label: Multiple
sections.description: Enable multiple selection mode using the ⁠multiple property to allow users to select more than one value from the autocomplete. When enabled, the value reference must be an array.

sections.label: Objects
sections.description: AutoComplete can also work with objects using the optionLabel property that defines the label to display as a suggestion. The value passed to the model would still be the object instance of a suggestion. Here is an example with a Country object that has name and code fields such as {name: "United States", code:"USA"} .

sections.label: reactive-forms-doc
sections.description: AutoComplete can also be used with reactive forms. In this case, the formControlName property is used to bind the component to a form control.

sections.label: Sizes
sections.description: AutoComplete provides small and large sizes as alternatives to the base.

sections.label: Template
sections.description: AutoComplete offers multiple templates for customization through templating.

sections.label: virtual-scroll-doc
sections.description: Virtual scrolling is an efficient way of rendering the options by displaying a small subset of data in the viewport at any time. When dealing with huge number of options, it is suggested to enable virtual scrolling to avoid performance issues. Usage is simple as setting virtualScroll property to true and defining virtualScrollItemSize to specify the height of an item.

props.name: minLength
props.description: Minimum number of characters to initiate a search.

props.name: minQueryLength
props.description: Minimum number of characters to initiate a search.

props.name: delay
props.description: Delay between keystrokes to wait before sending a query.

props.description: Inline style of the overlay panel element.

props.name: styleClass
props.description: Style class of the component.

props.name: panelStyleClass

props.name: inputStyle
props.description: Inline style of the input field.

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Hint text for the input field.

props.name: readonly
props.description: When present, it specifies that the input cannot be typed.

props.name: scrollHeight
props.description: Maximum height of the suggestions panel.

props.name: lazy
props.description: Defines if data is loaded and interacted with in lazy manner.

props.name: virtualScroll
props.description: Whether the data should be loaded on demand during scroll.

props.name: virtualScrollItemSize
props.description: Height of an item in the list for VirtualScrolling.

props.name: virtualScrollOptions
props.description: Whether to use the scroller feature. The properties of scroller component can be used like an object in it.

props.name: autoHighlight
props.description: When enabled, highlights the first item in the list by default.

props.name: forceSelection
props.description: When present, autocomplete clears the manual input if it does not match of the suggestions to force only accepting values from the suggestions.

props.name: type
props.description: Type of the input, defaults to "text".

props.name: autoZIndex
props.description: Whether to automatically manage layering.

props.name: baseZIndex
props.description: Base zIndex value to use in layering.

props.name: ariaLabel
props.description: Defines a string that labels the input for accessibility.

props.name: dropdownAriaLabel
props.description: Defines a string that labels the dropdown button for accessibility.

props.name: ariaLabelledBy
props.description: Specifies one or more IDs in the DOM that labels the input field.

props.name: dropdownIcon
props.description: Icon class of the dropdown icon.

props.name: unique
props.description: Ensures uniqueness of selected items on multiple mode.

props.name: group
props.description: Whether to display options as grouped when nested options are provided.

props.name: completeOnFocus
props.description: Whether to run a query when input receives focus.

props.name: showClear
props.description: When enabled, a clear icon is displayed to clear the value.

props.name: dropdown
props.description: Displays a button next to the input field when enabled.

props.name: showEmptyMessage
props.description: Whether to show the empty message or not.

props.name: dropdownMode
props.description: Specifies the behavior dropdown button. Default "blank" mode sends an empty string and "current" mode sends the input value.

props.name: multiple
props.description: Specifies if multiple values can be selected.

props.name: addOnTab
props.description: When enabled, the input value is added to the selected items on tab key press when multiple is true and typeahead is false.

props.name: tabindex
props.description: Index of the element in tabbing order.

props.name: dataKey
props.description: A property to uniquely identify a value in options.

props.name: emptyMessage
props.description: Text to display when there is no data. Defaults to global value in i18n translation configuration.

props.name: showTransitionOptions
props.description: Transition options of the show animation.

props.name: hideTransitionOptions
props.description: Transition options of the hide animation.

props.name: autofocus
props.description: When present, it specifies that the component should automatically get focus on load.

props.name: autocomplete
props.description: Used to define a string that autocomplete attribute the current element.

props.name: optionGroupChildren
props.description: Name of the options field of an option group.

props.name: optionGroupLabel
props.description: Name of the label field of an option group.

props.name: overlayOptions
props.description: Options for the overlay element.

props.name: suggestions
props.description: An array of suggestions to display.

props.name: optionLabel
props.description: Property name or getter function to use as the label of an option.

props.name: optionValue
props.description: Property name or getter function to use as the value of an option.

props.name: id
props.description: Unique identifier of the component.

props.name: searchMessage
props.description: Text to display when the search is active. Defaults to global value in i18n translation configuration.

props.name: emptySelectionMessage
props.description: Text to display when filtering does not return any results. Defaults to global value in i18n translation configuration.

props.name: selectionMessage
props.description: Text to be displayed in hidden accessible field when options are selected. Defaults to global value in i18n translation configuration.

props.name: autoOptionFocus
props.description: Whether to focus on the first visible or selected element when the overlay panel is shown.

props.name: selectOnFocus
props.description: When enabled, the focused option is selected.

props.name: searchLocale
props.description: Locale to use in searching. The default locale is the host environment's current locale.

props.name: optionDisabled
props.description: Property name or getter function to use as the disabled flag of an option, defaults to false when not defined.

props.name: focusOnHover
props.description: When enabled, the hovered option will be focused.

props.name: typeahead
props.description: Whether typeahead is active or not.

props.name: addOnBlur
props.description: Whether to add an item on blur event if the input has value and typeahead is false with multiple mode.

props.name: separator
props.description: Separator char to add item when typeahead is false and multiple mode is enabled.

props.name: appendTo
props.description: Target element to attach the overlay, valid values are "body" or a local ng-template variable of another element (note: use binding with brackets for template variables, e.g. [appendTo]="mydiv" for a div element having #mydiv as variable name).

props.name: motionOptions
props.description: The motion options.

props.name: dt
props.description: Defines scoped design tokens of the component.

props.name: unstyled
props.description: Indicates whether the component should be rendered without styles.

props.name: pt
props.description: Used to pass attributes to DOM elements inside the component.

props.name: ptOptions
props.description: Used to configure passthrough(pt) options of the component.

props.name: required
props.description: There must be a value (if set).

props.name: invalid
props.description: When present, it specifies that the component should have invalid state style.

props.name: disabled
props.description: When present, it specifies that the component should have disabled state style.

props.name: name
props.description: When present, it specifies that the name of the input.

props.name: fluid
props.description: Spans 100% width of the container when enabled.

props.name: variant
props.description: Specifies the input variant of the component.

props.name: size
props.description: Specifies the size of the component.

props.name: inputSize
props.description: Specifies the visible width of the input element in characters.

props.name: pattern
props.description: Specifies the value must match the pattern.

props.name: min
props.description: The value must be greater than or equal to the value.

props.name: max
props.description: The value must be less than or equal to the value.

props.name: step
props.description: Unless the step is set to the any literal, the value must be min + an integral multiple of the step.

props.name: minlength
props.description: The number of characters (code points) must not be less than the value of the attribute, if non-empty.

props.name: maxlength
props.description: The number of characters (code points) must not exceed the value of the attribute.

emits.name: completeMethod
emits.description: Callback to invoke to search for suggestions.

emits.description: Callback to invoke when a suggestion is selected.

components.name: avatar
components.title: Angular Avatar Component
components.description: Avatar represents people using icons, labels and images.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader Avatar does not include any roles and attributes by default. Any attribute is passed to the root element so you may add a role like img along with aria-labelledby or aria-label to describe the component. In case avatars need to be tabbable, tabIndex can be added as well to implement custom key handlers. Keyboard Support Component does not include any interactive elements.

sections.label: AvatarGroup
sections.description: Grouping is available by wrapping multiple Avatar components inside an AvatarGroup .

components.title: Angular Button Component
components.description: Button is an extension to standard button element with icons and theming.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader Button component renders a native button element that implicitly includes any passed prop. Text to describe the button is defined with the aria-label prop, if not present label prop is used as the value. If the button is icon only or custom templating is used, it is recommended to use aria-label so that screen readers would be able to read the element properly.

components.name: chart
components.title: Angular Chart Component
components.description: Chart components are based on Charts.js 3.3.2+, an open source HTML5 based charting library.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader Chart components internally use canvas element, refer to the Chart.js accessibility guide for more information.

sections.label: Basic
sections.description: A chart is configured with 3 properties; type , data and options . Chart type is defined using the type property that accepts pie , doughtnut , line , bar , radar and polarArea as a value. The data defines datasets represented with the chart and the options provide numerous customization options to customize the presentation.

sections.label: Chart.js
sections.description: To begin with, first you must install the charts.js package using npm and then include it in your project. An example with CLI would be;

sections.label: Combo
sections.description: Different chart types can be combined in the same graph using the type option of a dataset.

sections.label: Doughnut
sections.description: A doughnut chart is a variant of the pie chart, with a blank center allowing for additional information about the data as a whole to be included.

sections.label: Horizontal Bar
sections.description: A bar chart is rendered horizontally when indexAxis option is set as y .

sections.label: Line
sections.description: A line chart or line graph is a type of chart which displays information as a series of data points called 'markers' connected by straight line segments.

sections.label: linestyle-doc
sections.description: Various styles of a line series can be customized to display customizations like an area chart.

examples.typescript:
import { Component } from '@angular/core'; @Component({ template: `
Name Parameters Description
refresh - Redraws the graph with new data.
reinit - Destroys the graph first and then creates it again.
generateLegend - Returns an HTML string of a legend for that chart. The legend is generated from the legendCallback in the options.
`, standalone: true, imports: [] }) export class ChartMethodsDemo {}

sections.label: MultiAxis
sections.description: Multiple axes can be added using the scales option.

sections.label: Pie
sections.description: A pie chart is a circular statistical graphic which is divided into slices to illustrate numerical proportion.

sections.label: Polar Area
sections.description: Polar area charts are similar to pie charts, but each segment has the same angle - the radius of the segment differs depending on the value.

examples.typescript:
import { Component } from '@angular/core'; @Component({ template: `
Name Type Default Description
type string null Type of the chart.
data any null Data to display.
options any null Options to customize the chart.
plugins any[] null Array of per-chart plugins to customize the chart behaviour.
width string null Width of the chart.
height string null Height of the chart.
responsive boolean true Whether the chart is redrawn on screen size change.
onDataSelect function null Callback to execute when an element on chart is clicked.
`, standalone: true, imports: [] }) export class ChartPropsDemo {}

sections.label: radar-doc
sections.description: A radar chart is a graphical method of displaying multivariate data in the form of a two-dimensional chart of three or more quantitative variables represented on axes starting from the same point.

sections.label: Stacked Bar
sections.description: Bars can be stacked on top of each other when stacked option of a scale is enabled.

sections.label: Vertical Bar
sections.description: A bar chart or bar graph is a chart that presents grouped data with rectangular bars with lengths proportional to the values that they represent.

components.name: checkbox
components.title: Angular Checkbox Component
components.description: Checkbox is an extension to standard checkbox element with theming.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader Checkbox component uses a hidden native checkbox element internally that is only visible to screen readers. Value to describe the component can either be provided via label tag combined with inputId prop or using ariaLabelledBy , ariaLabel props.

sections.label: Basic
sections.description: Binary checkbox is used as a controlled input with ngModel and binary properties.

sections.label: Disabled
sections.description: When disabled is present, the element cannot be edited and focused.

sections.label: Dynamic
sections.description: Checkboxes can be generated using a list of values.

sections.label: Filled
sections.description: Specify the variant property as filled to display the component with a higher visual emphasis than the default outlined style.

sections.label: Indeterminate
sections.description: The indeterminate state indicates that a checkbox is neither "on" or "off".

sections.label: Invalid
sections.description: The invalid state is applied using the ⁠invalid property to indicate failed validation, which can be integrated with Angular Forms.

sections.label: label-doc
sections.description: The label attribute provides a label text for the checkbox. This label is also clickable and toggles the checked state.

sections.label: multiple-doc
sections.description: Multiple checkboxes can be grouped together.

examples.typescript:
import { Component } from '@angular/core'; import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms'; @Component({ template: `
`, standalone: true, imports: [FormsModule] }) export class CheckboxMultipleDemo { pizza: string[] = []; }

sections.label: reactiveforms-doc
sections.description: Checkbox can also be used with reactive forms. In this case, the formControlName property is used to bind the component to a form control.

sections.label: Sizes
sections.description: Checkbox provides small and large sizes as alternatives to the base.

components.description: ColorPicker is an input component to select a color.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader Specification does not cover a color picker yet and using a semantic native color picker is not consistent across browsers so currently component is not compatible with screen readers. In the upcoming versions, text fields will be introduced below the slider section to be able to pick a color using accessible text boxes in hsl, rgba and hex formats.

sections.label: Basic
sections.description: ColorPicker is used as a controlled input with ngModel property.

sections.label: Disabled
sections.description: When disabled is present, the element cannot be edited and focused.

sections.label: Format
sections.description: Default color format to use in value binding is hex and other possible values can be rgb and hsb using the format property.

sections.label: Inline
sections.description: ColorPicker is displayed as a popup by default, add inline property to customize this behavior.

sections.label: reactiveforms-doc
sections.description: ColorPicker can also be used with reactive forms. In this case, the formControlName property is used to bind the component to a form control.

components.name: confirmdialog
components.title: Angular ConfirmDialog Component
components.description: ConfirmDialog is backed by a service utilizing Observables to display confirmation windows easily that can be shared by multiple actions on the same component.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader ConfirmDialog component uses alertdialog role along with aria-labelledby referring to the header element however any attribute is passed to the root element so you may use aria-labelledby to override this default behavior. In addition aria-modal is added since focus is kept within the popup. It is recommended to use a trigger component that can be accessed with keyboard such as a button, if not adding tabIndex would be necessary. When confirm function is used and a trigger is passed as a parameter, ConfirmDialog adds aria-expanded state attribute and aria-controls to the trigger so that the relation between the trigger and the popup is defined. If the dialog is controlled with the visible property aria-expanded and aria-controls need to be handled explicitly. Overlay Keyboard Support Key Function tab Moves focus to the next the focusable element within the popup. shift + tab Moves focus to the previous the focusable element within the popup. escape Closes the popup and moves focus to the trigger. Buttons Keyboard Support Key Function enter Triggers the action, closes the popup and moves focus to the trigger. space Triggers the action, closes the popup and moves focus to the trigger.

sections.label: Basic
sections.description: ConfirmDialog is defined using p-confirmdialog tag and an instance of ConfirmationService is required to display it bycalling confirm method.

sections.label: Headless
sections.description: Headless mode allows you to customize the entire user interface instead of the default elements.

sections.label: Position
sections.description: The position property of the confirm options is used to display a Dialog at all edges and corners of the screen.

sections.label: Template
sections.description: Properties of the dialog are defined in two ways, message , icon , header properties can either be defined using confirm method or declaratively on p-confirmDialog ng-template by header , message , icon and footer templates. If these values are unlikely to change then declarative approach would be useful, still properties defined in a ng-template can be overridden with confirm method call. In addition, buttons at footer section can be customized by passing your own UI, important note to make confirmation work with a custom UI is defining a local ng-template variable for the dialog and assign accept()-reject() methods to your own buttons.

components.name: dataview
components.title: Angular DataView Component
components.description: DataView displays data in grid grid-cols-12 gap-4 or list layout with pagination and sorting features.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader The container element that wraps the layout options buttons has a group role whereas each button element uses button role and aria-pressed is updated depending on selection state. Values to describe the buttons are derived from the aria.listView and aria.gridView properties of the locale API respectively. Refer to paginator accessibility documentation for the paginator of the component. Keyboard Support Key Function tab Moves focus to the buttons. space Toggles the checked state of a button.

sections.label: Basic
sections.description: DataView requires a value to display along with a list template that receives an object in the collection to return content.

sections.label: Layout
sections.description: DataView supports list and grid display modes defined with the layout property. The grid mode is not built-in for flexibility purposes and requires a library with CSS grid features like Tailwind.

examples.typescript:
import { Component, OnInit, inject, signal } from '@angular/core'; import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms'; import { ProductService } from '@/service/productservice'; import { Product } from '@/domain/product'; @Component({ template: `
{{ item.category }}
{{ item.name }}
{{ item.rating }}
{{ item.price | currency: 'USD' }}
{{ product.category }}
{{ product.name }}
{{ product.rating }}
{{ product.price | currency: 'USD' }}
`, standalone: true, imports: [FormsModule], providers: [ProductService] }) export class DataviewLayoutDemo implements OnInit { private productService = inject(ProductService); products = signal([]); options: any[] = ['list', 'grid']; ngOnInit() { this.productService.getProducts().then((data) => { this.products.set([...data.slice(0, 12)]); }); } }

sections.label: Pagination
sections.description: Pagination is enabled with the paginator and rows properties. Refer to the Paginator for more information about customizing the paginator.

examples.typescript:
import { Component, OnInit, inject, signal } from '@angular/core'; import { ProductService } from '@/service/productservice'; import { Product } from '@/domain/product'; @Component({ template: `
{{ item.category }}
{{ item.name }}
{{ item.rating }}
{{ '$' + item.price }}
`, standalone: true, imports: [], providers: [ProductService] }) export class DataviewPaginationDemo implements OnInit { private productService = inject(ProductService); products = signal([]); ngOnInit() { this.productService.getProducts().then((data) => { this.products.set(data); }); } }

sections.label: Sorting
sections.description: Built-in sorting is controlled by bindings sortField and sortOrder properties from a custom UI.

components.name: datepicker
components.title: Angular DatePicker Component
components.description: DatePicker is an input component to select a date.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader Value to describe the component can either be provided via label tag combined with inputId prop or using aria-labelledby , aria-label props. The input element has combobox role in addition to aria-autocomplete as "none", aria-haspopup as "dialog" and aria-expanded attributes. The relation between the input and the popup is created with aria-controls attribute that refers to the id of the popup. The optional DatePicker button requires includes aria-haspopup , aria-expanded for states along with aria-controls to define the relation between the popup and the button. The value to read is retrieved from the chooseDate key of the aria property from the locale API. This label is also used for the aria-label of the popup as well. When there is a value selected, it is formatted and appended to the label to be able to notify users about the current value. Popup has a dialog role along with aria-modal and aria-label . The navigation buttons at the header has an aria-label retrieved from the prevYear , nextYear , prevMonth , nextMonth , prevDecade and nextDecade keys of the locale aria API. Similarly month picker button uses the chooseMonth and year picker button uses the chooseYear keys. Main date table uses grid role that contains th elements with col as the scope along with abbr tag resolving to the full name of the month. Each date cell has an aria-label referring to the full date value. Buttons at the footer utilize their readable labels as aria-label as well. Selected date also receives the aria-selected attribute. Timepicker spinner buttons get their labels for aria-label from the aria locale API using the prevHour , nextHour , prevMinute , nextMinute , prevSecond , nextSecond , am and pm keys. DatePicker also includes a hidden section that is only available to screen readers with aria-live as "polite". This element is updated when the selected date changes to instruct the user about the current date selected.

sections.label: Basic
sections.description: Two-way value binding is defined using the standard ngModel directive referencing to a Date property.

sections.label: Button Bar
sections.description: When showButtonBar is present, today and clear buttons are displayed at the footer. The content can be fully customized with the buttonbar template as well.

sections.label: Clear Icon
sections.description: When showClear is enabled, a clear icon is displayed to clear the value.

sections.label: Date Template
sections.description: Custom content can be placed inside date cells with the ng-template property that takes a Date as a parameter.

sections.label: Disabled
sections.description: When disabled is present, the element cannot be edited and focused.

examples.typescript:
import { Component } from '@angular/core'; @Component({ template: `
Name Parameters Description
onSelect value: Selected value Callback to invoke when a date is selected. Note that this event is not called when the value is entered from the input manually.
onBlur event: Blur event Callback to invoke on blur of input field.
onFocus event: Focus event Callback to invoke on focus of input field.
onClose event: Close event Callback to invoke when datepicker panel is closed.
onShow event: Animation event Callback to invoke when datepicker panel is visible.
onClickOutside event: Click event Callback to invoke when click outside of datepicker panel.
onInput event: Input event Callback to invoke when input field is being typed.
onTodayClick event: Click event Callback to invoke when today button is clicked.
onClearClick event: Click event Callback to invoke when clear button is clicked.
onMonthChange event.month: New month
event.year: New year 		Callback to invoke when a month is changed using the navigators.
onYearChange event.month: New month
event.year: New year 		Callback to invoke when a year is changed using the navigators.
onClear - Callback to invoke when input field is cleared.
`, standalone: true, imports: [] }) export class DatepickerEventsDemo {}

sections.label: Filled
sections.description: Specify the variant property as filled to display the component with a higher visual emphasis than the default outlined style.

sections.label: Float Label
sections.description: A floating label appears on top of the input field when focused. Visit FloatLabel documentation for more information.

sections.label: Fluid
sections.description: The fluid prop makes the component take up the full width of its container when set to true.

sections.label: Format
sections.description: Default date format is mm/dd/yy which can be customized using the dateFormat property. Following options can be a part of the format. d - day of month (no leading zero) dd - day of month (two digit) o - day of the year (no leading zeros) oo - day of the year (three digit) D - day name short DD - day name long m - month of year (no leading zero) mm - month of year (two digit) M - month name short MM - month name long y - year (two digit) yy - year (four digit) @ - Unix timestamp (ms since 01/01/1970) ! - Windows ticks (100ns since 01/01/0001) '...' - literal text '' - single quote anything else - literal text

sections.label: Icon
sections.description: An additional icon is displayed next to the input field when showIcon is present.

sections.label: Inline
sections.description: DatePicker is displayed as a popup by default, add inline property to customize this behavior.

sections.label: Invalid
sections.description: The invalid state is applied using the ⁠invalid property to indicate failed validation, which can be integrated with Angular Forms.

sections.label: Locale
sections.description: Locale for different languages and formats is defined globally, refer to the VoxxUI Locale configuration for more information.

sections.label: Mask
sections.description: DatePicker can be used with the vxInputMask directive to enforce a specific input format.

examples.typescript:
import { Component } from '@angular/core'; @Component({ template: `
Name Parameters Description
toggle - Toggles the visibility of the calendar.
`, standalone: true, imports: [] }) export class DatepickerMethodsDemo {}

sections.label: Min / Max
sections.description: Boundaries for the permitted dates that can be entered are defined with minDate and maxDate properties.

sections.label: month-doc
sections.description: Month only picker is enabled by specifying view as month in addition to a suitable dateFormat .

sections.label: Multiple
sections.description: In order to choose multiple dates, set selectionMode as multiple . In this mode, the value binding should be an array.

sections.label: Multiple Months
sections.description: Number of months to display is configured with the numberOfMonths property.

sections.label: Range
sections.description: A range of dates can be selected by defining selectionMode as range , in this case the bound value would be an array with two values where first date is the start of the range and second date is the end.

sections.label: reactiveforms-doc
sections.description: DatePicker can also be used with reactive forms. In this case, the formControlName property is used to bind the component to a form control.

sections.label: Sizes
sections.description: DatePicker provides small and large sizes as alternatives to the base.

sections.label: template-doc
sections.description: Calendar UI accepts custom content using header and footer templates.

examples.typescript:
import { Component } from '@angular/core'; @Component({ template: `
Name Parameters
header -
footer -
date $implicit: Value of the component
decade $implicit: An array containing the start and and year of a decade to display at header of the year picker.
previousicon -
nexticon -
triggericon -
clearicon -
incrementicon -
decrementicon -
`, standalone: true, imports: [] }) export class DatepickerTemplatesDemo {}

sections.label: Time
sections.description: A time picker is displayed when showTime is enabled where 12/24 hour format is configured with hourFormat property. In case, only time needs to be selected, add timeOnly to hide the date section.

sections.label: touchui-doc
sections.description: When touchUI is enabled, overlay is displayed as optimized for touch devices.

sections.label: year-doc
sections.description: Specifying view as year in addition to a suitable dateFormat enables the year picker.

props.description: Separator for multiple selection mode.

props.name: rangeSeparator
props.description: Separator for joining start and end dates on range selection mode.

props.name: inline
props.description: When enabled, displays the datepicker as inline. Default is false for popup mode.

props.name: showOtherMonths
props.description: Whether to display dates in other months (non-selectable) at the start or end of the current month. To make these days selectable use the selectOtherMonths option.

props.name: selectOtherMonths
props.description: Whether days in other months shown before or after the current month are selectable. This only applies if the showOtherMonths option is set to true.

props.name: showIcon
props.description: When enabled, displays a button with icon next to input.

props.name: icon
props.description: Icon of the datepicker button.

props.name: readonlyInput
props.description: When specified, prevents entering the date manually with keyboard.

props.name: shortYearCutoff
props.description: The cutoff year for determining the century for a date.

props.name: hourFormat
props.description: Specifies 12 or 24 hour format.

props.name: timeOnly
props.description: Whether to display timepicker only.

props.name: stepHour
props.description: Hours to change per step.

props.name: stepMinute
props.description: Minutes to change per step.

props.name: stepSecond
props.description: Seconds to change per step.

props.name: showSeconds
props.description: Whether to show the seconds in time picker.

props.name: showOnFocus
props.description: When disabled, datepicker will not be visible with input focus.

props.name: showWeek
props.description: When enabled, datepicker will show week numbers.

props.name: startWeekFromFirstDayOfYear
props.description: When enabled, datepicker will start week numbers from first day of the year.

props.name: showClear
props.description: When enabled, a clear icon is displayed to clear the value.

props.name: dataType
props.description: Type of the value to write back to ngModel, default is date and alternative is string.

props.name: selectionMode
props.description: Defines the quantity of the selection, valid values are "single", "multiple" and "range".

props.name: maxDateCount
props.description: Maximum number of selectable dates in multiple mode.

props.name: showButtonBar
props.description: Whether to display today and clear buttons at the footer

props.name: todayButtonStyleClass
props.description: Style class of the today button.

props.name: clearButtonStyleClass
props.description: Style class of the clear button.

props.name: autofocus
props.description: When present, it specifies that the component should automatically get focus on load.

props.name: autoZIndex
props.description: Whether to automatically manage layering.

props.name: baseZIndex
props.description: Base zIndex value to use in layering.

props.name: panelStyleClass
props.description: Style class of the datetimepicker container element.

props.name: panelStyle
props.description: Inline style of the datetimepicker container element.

props.name: keepInvalid
props.description: Keep invalid value when input blur.

props.name: hideOnDateTimeSelect
props.description: Whether to hide the overlay on date selection.

props.name: touchUI
props.description: When enabled, datepicker overlay is displayed as optimized for touch devices.

props.name: timeSeparator
props.description: Separator of time selector.

props.name: focusTrap
props.description: When enabled, can only focus on elements inside the datepicker.

props.name: showTransitionOptions
props.description: Transition options of the show animation.

props.name: hideTransitionOptions
props.description: Transition options of the hide animation.

props.name: tabindex
props.description: Index of the element in tabbing order.

props.name: minDate
props.description: The minimum selectable date.

props.name: maxDate
props.description: The maximum selectable date.

props.name: disabledDates
props.description: Array with dates that should be disabled (not selectable).

props.name: disabledDays
props.description: Array with weekday numbers that should be disabled (not selectable).

props.name: showTime
props.description: Whether to display timepicker.

props.name: responsiveOptions
props.description: An array of options for responsive design.

props.name: numberOfMonths
props.description: Number of months to display.

props.name: firstDayOfWeek
props.description: Defines the first of the week for various date calculations.

props.name: view
props.description: Type of view to display, valid values are "date" for datepicker and "month" for month picker.

props.name: defaultDate
props.description: Set the date to highlight on first opening if the field is blank.

props.name: appendTo
props.description: Target element to attach the overlay, valid values are "body" or a local ng-template variable of another element (note: use binding with brackets for template variables, e.g. [appendTo]="mydiv" for a div element having #mydiv as variable name).

props.name: motionOptions
props.description: The motion options.

components.name: dialog
components.title: Angular Dialog Component
components.description: Dialog is a container to display content in an overlay window.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader Dialog component uses dialog role along with aria-labelledby referring to the header element however any attribute is passed to the root element so you may use aria-labelledby to override this default behavior. In addition aria-modal is added since focus is kept within the popup. It is recommended to use a trigger component that can be accessed with keyboard such as a button, if not adding tabIndex would be necessary. Trigger element also requires aria-expanded and aria-controls to be handled explicitly. Close element is a button with an aria-label that refers to the aria.close property of the locale API by default, you may use closeButtonProps to customize the element and override the default aria-label . Maximize element is a button with an aria-label that refers to the aria.maximizeLabel and aria.minimizeLabel property of the locale API. It cannot be customized using the maximizeButtonProps . Overlay Keyboard Support Key Function tab Moves focus to the next the focusable element within the dialog. shift + tab Moves focus to the previous the focusable element within the dialog. escape Closes the dialog if closeOnEscape is true. Close Button Keyboard Support Key Function enter Closes the dialog. space Closes the dialog.

sections.label: Basic
sections.description: Dialog is used as a container and visibility is controlled with visible property.

sections.label: Headless
sections.description: Headless mode allows you to customize the entire user interface instead of the default elements.

examples.typescript:
import { Component } from '@angular/core'; @Component({ template: `
`, standalone: true, imports: [] }) export class DialogHeadlessDemo { visible: boolean = false; showDialog() { this.visible = true; } closeDialog() { this.visible = false; } }

sections.label: Long Content
sections.description: Dialog automatically displays a scroller when content exceeds viewport.

sections.label: Maximizable
sections.description: Setting maximizable property to true enables the full screen mode.

sections.label: modal-doc
sections.description: Mask layer behind the Dialog can be turned on by setting the modal property to true .

sections.label: overlaysinside-doc
sections.description: When dialog includes other components with overlays such as dropdown, the overlay part cannot exceed dialog boundaries due to overflow. In order to solve this, you can either append the overlay to the body by using appendTo property or allow overflow in dialog.

sections.label: Position
sections.description: The position property is used to display a Dialog at all edges and corners of the screen.

examples.typescript:
import { Component } from '@angular/core'; import { Dialog } from 'voxx-ui/dialog'; @Component({ template: `
Update your information.
`, standalone: true, imports: [] }) export class DialogPositionDemo { visible: boolean = false; showDialog(position: 'left' | 'right' | 'top' | 'bottom' | 'center' | 'topleft' | 'topright' | 'bottomleft' | 'bottomright') { this.position = position; this.visible = true; } }

sections.label: Responsive
sections.description: Dialog width can be adjusted per screen size with the breakpoints option where a key defines the max-width for the breakpoint and value for the corresponding width. When no breakpoint matches width defined in style property is used.

sections.label: Template
sections.description: Dialog can be customized using header and footer templates.

examples.typescript:
import { Component } from '@angular/core'; @Component({ template: `
Amy Elsner
Update your information.
`, standalone: true, imports: [] }) export class DialogTemplateDemo { visible: boolean = false; showDialog() { this.visible = true; } }

sections.label: Without Modal
sections.description: Mask layer behind the Dialog is configured with the modal property. By default, no modal layer is added.

components.name: drawer
components.title: Angular Drawer Component
components.description: Drawer is a container component displayed as an overlay.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader Drawer component uses complementary role by default, since any attribute is passed to the root element aria role can be changed depending on your use case and additional attributes like aria-labelledby can be added. In addition aria-modal is added since focus is kept within the drawer when opened. It is recommended to use a trigger component that can be accessed with keyboard such as a button, if not adding tabIndex would be necessary. Trigger element also requires aria-expanded and aria-controls to be handled explicitly. Overlay Keyboard Support Key Function tab Moves focus to the next the focusable element within the drawer. shift + tab Moves focus to the previous the focusable element within the drawer. escape Closes the dialog if closeOnEscape is true. Close Button Keyboard Support Key Function enter Closes the drawer. space Closes the drawer.

sections.label: Basic
sections.description: Drawer is used as a container and visibility is controlled with a binding to visible .

sections.label: Full Screen
sections.description: Drawer can cover the whole page when fullScreen property is enabled.

sections.label: Headless
sections.description: Headless mode allows you to customize the entire user interface instead of the default elements.

examples.typescript:
import { Component } from '@angular/core'; import { Drawer } from 'voxx-ui/drawer'; @Component({ template: `
Your Logo
  • FAVORITES
    • Dashboard
    • Bookmarks
    • Reports
      • Revenue
        • View
        • Search
      • Expenses
    • Team
    • Messages 3
    • Calendar
    • Settings
  • APPLICATION
    • Projects
    • Performance
    • Settings
Amy Elsner
`, standalone: true, imports: [] }) export class DrawerHeadlessDemo { visible: boolean = false; closeCallback(e): void { this.drawerRef.close(e); } }

sections.label: Position
sections.description: Drawer location is configured with the position property that can take left , right , top and bottom as a value.

components.name: dynamicdialog
components.title: Angular Dynamic Dialog Component
components.description: Dialogs can be created dynamically with any component as the content using a DialogService.

sections.label: Closing a Dialog
sections.description: Most of the time, requirement is returning a value from the dialog. DialogRef's close method is used for this purpose where the parameter passed will be available at the onClose event at the caller. Here is an example on how to close the dialog from the ProductListDemo by passing a selected product.

sections.label: Customization
sections.description: DynamicDialog uses the Dialog component internally, visit dialog for more information about the available props.

sections.label: Example
sections.description: Dynamic dialogs require an instance of a DialogService that is responsible for displaying a dialog with a component as its content. Calling open method of DialogService will display dynamic dialog. First parameter of open method is the type of component to load and the second parameter is the configuration of the Dialog such as header , width and more.

sections.label: Opening a Dialog
sections.description: The open method of the DialogService is used to open a Dialog. First parameter is the component to load and second one is the configuration object to customize the Dialog.

sections.label: Passing Data
sections.description: To pass data to a dynamically loaded component, you can use either the data or inputValues property, depending on your requirements. The data property is ideal for passing generic information that is not directly tied to the component's inputs, while inputValues allows you to set specific input properties on the component in a more structured and type-safe way. Both properties can be used together or independently, offering flexibility to meet different use cases. Additionally, the loaded component can control the dialog using the DynamicDialogRef API, providing complete control over the dialog lifecycle. Both DynamicDialogConfig and DynamicDialogRef are injectable through the constructor.

sections.label: style-doc
sections.description: Following is the list of structural style classes, for theming classes visit theming page.

sections.label: Usage
sections.description: To use dynamic dialog, a reference should be declared as DynamicDialogRef after the DialogService injected into the component.

components.name: editor
components.title: Angular Editor Component
components.description: Editor is rich text editor component based on Quill.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Quill performs generally well in terms of accessibility. The elements in the toolbar can be tabbed and have the necessary ARIA roles/attributes for screen readers. One known limitation is the lack of arrow key support for dropdowns in the toolbar that may be overcome with a custom toolbar.

sections.label: Basic
sections.description: A model can be bound using the standard ngModel directive.

sections.label: customtoolbar-doc
sections.description: Editor provides a default toolbar with common options, to customize it define your elements inside the header element. Refer to Quill documentation for available controls.

sections.label: Quill
sections.description: Editor uses Quill editor underneath so it needs to be installed as a dependency.

sections.label: reactiveforms-doc
sections.description: Editor can also be used with reactive forms. In this case, the formControlName property is used to bind the component to a form control.

sections.label: ReadOnly
sections.description: When readonly is present, the value cannot be edited.

components.name: fileupload
components.title: Angular FileUpload Component
components.description: FileUpload is an advanced uploader with dragdrop support, multi file uploads, auto uploading, progress tracking and validations.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader FileUpload uses a hidden native input element with type="file" for screen readers. Keyboard Support Interactive elements of the uploader are buttons, visit the Button accessibility section for more information.

sections.label: Advanced
sections.description: Advanced uploader provides dragdrop support, multi file uploads, auto uploading, progress tracking and validations.

sections.label: Auto
sections.description: When auto property is enabled, a file gets uploaded instantly after selection.

sections.label: Basic
sections.description: FileUpload basic mode provides a simpler UI as an alternative to default advanced mode.

sections.label: custom-doc
sections.description: FileUpload basic mode provides a simpler UI as an alternative to default advanced mode.

components.name: fluid
components.title: Angular Fluid Component
components.description: Fluid is a layout component to make descendant components span full width of their container.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader Fluid does not require any roles and attributes. Keyboard Support Component does not include any interactive elements.

sections.label: Basic
sections.description: Components with the fluid option like InputText have the ability to span the full width of their component. Enabling the fluid for each component individually may be cumbersome so wrap the content with Fluid to instead for an easier alternative. Any component that has the fluid property can be nested inside the Fluid component. The fluid property of a child component has higher precedence than the fluid container as shown in the last sample.

components.title: Angular InputMask Component
components.description: InputMask component is used to enter input in a certain format such as numeric, date, currency and phone.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader InputMask directive is used with a native input element that implicitly includes any passed attribute. Value to describe the component can either be provided via label tag combined with id attribute or using aria-labelledby , aria-label attributes.

sections.label: Basic
sections.description: InputMask is used as a controlled input with ngModel properties.

sections.label: clearicon-doc
sections.description: When showClear is enabled, a clear icon is displayed to clear the value.

sections.label: Disabled
sections.description: When disabled is present, the element cannot be edited and focused.

sections.label: Filled
sections.description: Specify the variant property as filled to display the component with a higher visual emphasis than the default outlined style.

sections.label: Float Label
sections.description: FloatLabel visually integrates a label with its form element. Visit FloatLabel documentation for more information.

sections.label: Fluid
sections.description: The fluid prop makes the component take up the full width of its container when set to true.

sections.label: Invalid
sections.description: The invalid state is applied using the ⁠invalid property to indicate failed validation, which can be integrated with Angular Forms.

sections.label: Mask
sections.description: Mask format can be a combination of the following definitions; a for alphabetic characters, 9 for numeric characters and * for alphanumberic characters. In addition, formatting characters like ( , ) , - are also accepted.

sections.label: Optional
sections.description: When the input does not complete the mask definition, it is cleared by default. Use autoClear property to control this behavior. In addition, ? is used to mark anything after the question mark optional.

sections.label: reactiveforms-doc
sections.description: InputMask can also be used with reactive forms. In this case, the formControlName property is used to bind the component to a form control.

sections.label: Sizes
sections.description: InputMask provides small and large sizes as alternatives to the base.

sections.label: SlotChar
sections.description: Default placeholder for a mask is underscore that can be customized using slotChar property.

components.name: inputnumber
components.title: Angular InputNumber Component
components.description: InputNumber is an input component to provide numerical input.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader Value to describe the component can either be provided via label tag combined with inputId prop or using ariaLabelledBy , ariaLabel , ariaDescribedBy props. The input element uses spinbutton role in addition to the aria-valuemin , aria-valuemax and aria-valuenow attributes.

sections.label: Buttons
sections.description: Spinner buttons are enabled using the showButtons options and layout is defined with the buttonLayout . Default value is "stacked" whereas "horizontal" and "stacked" are alternatives. Note that even there are no buttons, up and down arrow keys can be used to spin the values with keyboard.

examples.typescript:
import { Component } from '@angular/core'; import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms'; @Component({ template: `
`, standalone: true, imports: [FormsModule] }) export class InputnumberButtonsDemo { value1: number = 20; value2: number = 10.5; value3: number = 25; }

sections.label: Clear Icon
sections.description: When showClear is enabled, a clear icon is displayed to clear the value.

sections.label: Currency
sections.description: Currency formatting is specified by setting the mode option to currency and currency property. In addition currencyDisplay option allows how the currency is displayed, valid values are "symbol" (default) or "code".

sections.label: Disabled
sections.description: When disabled is present, the element cannot be edited and focused.

sections.label: Filled
sections.description: Specify the variant property as filled to display the component with a higher visual emphasis than the default outlined style.

sections.label: Float Label
sections.description: A floating label appears on top of the input field when focused. Visit FloatLabel documentation for more information.

sections.label: Fluid
sections.description: The fluid prop makes the component take up the full width of its container when set to true.

sections.label: Invalid
sections.description: The invalid state is applied using the ⁠invalid property to indicate failed validation, which can be integrated with Angular Forms.

sections.label: Locale
sections.description: Localization information such as grouping and decimal symbols are defined with the locale property which defaults to the user locale.

sections.label: Numerals
sections.description: InputNumber is used as a controlled input with ngModel property.

sections.label: Prefix & Suffix
sections.description: Custom texts e.g. units can be placed before or after the input section with the prefix and suffix properties.

sections.label: reactiveforms-doc
sections.description: InputNumber can also be used with reactive forms. In this case, the formControlName property is used to bind the component to a form control.

sections.label: Sizes
sections.description: InputNumber provides small and large sizes as alternatives to the base.

sections.label: Vertical
sections.description: Buttons can also placed vertically by setting buttonLayout as vertical .

props.description: Determines whether the input field is empty.

props.name: locale
props.description: Locale to be used in formatting.

props.name: localeMatcher
props.description: The locale matching algorithm to use. Possible values are "lookup" and "best fit"; the default is "best fit". See Locale Negotiation for details.

props.name: mode
props.description: Defines the behavior of the component, valid values are "decimal" and "currency".

props.name: currency
props.description: The currency to use in currency formatting. Possible values are the ISO 4217 currency codes, such as "USD" for the US dollar, "EUR" for the euro, or "CNY" for the Chinese RMB. There is no default value; if the style is "currency", the currency property must be provided.

props.name: currencyDisplay
props.description: How to display the currency in currency formatting. Possible values are "symbol" to use a localized currency symbol such as €, ü"code" to use the ISO currency code, "name" to use a localized currency name such as "dollar"; the default is "symbol".

props.name: useGrouping
props.description: Whether to use grouping separators, such as thousands separators or thousand/lakh/crore separators.

props.name: minFractionDigits
props.description: The minimum number of fraction digits to use. Possible values are from 0 to 20; the default for plain number and percent formatting is 0; the default for currency formatting is the number of minor unit digits provided by the ISO 4217 currency code list (2 if the list doesn't provide that information).

props.name: maxFractionDigits
props.description: The maximum number of fraction digits to use. Possible values are from 0 to 20; the default for plain number formatting is the larger of minimumFractionDigits and 3; the default for currency formatting is the larger of minimumFractionDigits and the number of minor unit digits provided by the ISO 4217 currency code list (2 if the list doesn't provide that information).

props.name: prefix
props.description: Text to display before the value.

props.name: suffix
props.description: Text to display after the value.

props.description: Inline style of the input field.

props.description: When present, it specifies that the component should automatically get focus on load.

components.name: inputtext
components.title: Angular InputText Component
components.description: InputText is an extension to standard input element with theming and keyfiltering.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader InputText component renders a native input element that implicitly includes any passed prop. Value to describe the component can either be provided via label tag combined with id prop or using aria-labelledby , aria-label props.

sections.label: Basic
sections.description: InputText is used as a controlled input with ngModel property.

sections.label: Disabled
sections.description: When disabled is present, the element cannot be edited and focused.

sections.label: Filled
sections.description: Specify the variant property as filled to display the component with a higher visual emphasis than the default outlined style.

sections.label: Float Label
sections.description: FloatLabel visually integrates a label with its form element. Visit FloatLabel documentation for more information.

sections.label: Fluid
sections.description: The fluid prop makes the component take up the full width of its container when set to true.

sections.label: Help Text
sections.description: An advisory text can be defined with the semantic small tag.

sections.label: icons-doc
sections.description: Icons can be placed inside an input element by wrapping both the input and the icon with an element that has either .p-input-icon-left or p-input-icon-right class.

sections.label: Invalid
sections.description: The invalid state is applied using the ⁠invalid property to indicate failed validation, which can be integrated with Angular Forms.

sections.label: Sizes
sections.description: InputText provides small and large sizes as alternatives to the standard.

components.name: listbox
components.title: Angular Listbox Component
components.description: Listbox is used to select one or more values from a list of items.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader Value to describe the component can be provided ariaLabelledBy or ariaLabel props. The list element has a listbox role with the aria-multiselectable attribute that sets to true when multiple selection is enabled. Each list item has an option role with aria-selected and aria-disabled as their attributes.

sections.label: Basic
sections.description: Listbox is used as a controlled component with ngModel property along with an options collection. Label and value of an option are defined with the optionLabel and optionValue properties respectively. Default property name for the optionLabel is label and value for the optionValue . If optionValue is omitted and the object has no value property, the object itself becomes the value of an option. Note that, when options are simple primitive values such as a string array, no optionLabel and optionValue would be necessary.

sections.label: Checkbox
sections.description: Listbox allows item selection using checkboxes.

sections.label: Checkmark
sections.description: An alternative way to highlight the selected option is displaying a checkmark instead.

sections.label: Disabled
sections.description: When disabled is present, the element cannot be edited and focused.

sections.label: dragdrop-doc
sections.description: Items can be reordered using drag and drop by enabling dragdrop property. Depends on @angular/cdk package.

sections.label: Filter
sections.description: ListBox provides built-in filtering that is enabled by adding the filter property.

sections.label: Group
sections.description: Options can be grouped when a nested data structures is provided.

sections.label: Invalid
sections.description: The invalid state is applied using the ⁠invalid property to indicate failed validation, which can be integrated with Angular Forms.

sections.label: Multiple
sections.description: ListBox allows choosing a single item by default, enable multiple property to choose more than one. When the optional metaKeySelection is present, behavior is changed in a way that selecting a new item requires meta key to be present.

sections.label: reactiveforms-doc
sections.description: Listbox can also be used with reactive forms. In this case, the formControlName property is used to bind the component to a form control.

sections.label: Template
sections.description: For custom content support define a template named item where the default local template variable refers to an option.

sections.label: Virtual Scroll
sections.description: VirtualScrolling is an efficient way of rendering the options by displaying a small subset of data in the viewport at any time. When dealing with huge number of options, it is suggested to enable VirtualScrolling to avoid performance issues. Usage is simple as setting virtualScroll property to true and defining virtualScrollItemSize to specify the height of an item.

components.name: llms
components.title: LLMs.txt - VoxxUI
components.description: LLM-optimized documentation endpoints for VoxxUI components.

sections.label: /llms-full.txt
sections.description: The llms-full.txt file is a complete list of all the pages in the VoxxUI documentation. It is used to help AI models understand the entire documentation set.

sections.label: /llms.txt
sections.description: The llms.txt file is an industry standard that helps AI models better understand and navigate the VoxxUI documentation. It lists key pages in a structured format, making it easier for LLMs to retrieve relevant information.

sections.label: markdownextension-doc
sections.description: Add a .md to a page's URL to display a Markdown version of that page.

components.name: mcp
components.title: MCP Server - VoxxUI
components.description: Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for VoxxUI component library. Provides AI assistants with comprehensive access to VoxxUI component documentation.

sections.label: Claude Code
sections.description: Add the VoxxUI MCP server using the CLI. After adding, start a new session and use /mcp to verify the connection.

sections.label: Cursor
sections.description: Create .cursor/mcp.json in your project or ~/.cursor/mcp.json for global configuration.

sections.label: exampleprompts-doc
sections.description: Once installed, try asking your AI assistant:

sections.label: Introduction
sections.description: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard that enables AI models to connect with external tools and data sources . The VoxxUI MCP server provides AI assistants with comprehensive access to: Component documentation including props , events , templates , and methods Theming and styling with Pass Through and design tokens Code examples and usage patterns Migration guides for version upgrades Installation and configuration guides

sections.label: OpenAI Codex
sections.description: Add the VoxxUI MCP server using the CLI or edit ~/.codex/config.toml directly.

sections.label: Available Tools
sections.description: Component Information Tools for exploring and understanding VoxxUI components. list_components : List all VoxxUI components with categories get_component : Get detailed info about a specific component search_components : Search components by name or description get_component_props : Get all props for a component get_component_events : Get all events for a component get_component_methods : Get all methods for a component get_component_slots : Get all templates for a component compare_components : Compare two components side by side Code Examples Tools for retrieving code samples and generating templates. get_usage_example : Get code examples for a component list_examples : List all available code examples get_example : Get a specific example by component and section generate_component_template : Generate a basic component template Theming & Styling Tools for customizing component appearance and styling. get_component_pt : Get Pass Through options for DOM customization get_component_tokens : Get design tokens (CSS variables) get_component_styles : Get CSS class names get_theming_guide : Get detailed theming guide get_passthrough_guide : Get Pass Through customization guide get_tailwind_guide : Get Tailwind CSS integration guide Documentation & Guides Tools for accessing VoxxUI documentation and guides. list_guides : List all guides and documentation pages get_guide : Get a specific guide by name get_configuration : Get VoxxUI configuration options get_installation : Get installation instructions get_accessibility_guide : Get accessibility guide get_accessibility_info : Get accessibility info for a component Migration Tools for upgrading between VoxxUI versions. get_migration_guide : Get migration guide overview migrate_v18_to_v19 : v18 to v19 migration guide migrate_v19_to_v20 : v19 to v20 migration guide migrate_v20_to_v21 : v20 to v21 migration guide Search & Discovery Tools for finding components based on various criteria. search_all : Search across components, guides, and props suggest_component : Suggest components based on use case find_by_prop : Find components with a specific prop find_by_event : Find components that emit a specific event find_components_with_feature : Find components supporting a feature get_related_components : Find related components

sections.label: VS Code
sections.description: Create .vscode/mcp.json in your project or ~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/mcp.json for global configuration.

sections.label: Windsurf
sections.description: Edit ~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json to add the VoxxUI MCP server.

sections.label: Zed
sections.description: Add to your Zed settings at ~/.config/zed/settings.json (Linux) or ~/Library/Application Support/Zed/settings.json (macOS).

components.name: menu
components.title: Angular Menu Component
components.description: Menu is a navigation / command component that supports dynamic and static positioning.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader Menu component uses the menu role and the value to describe the menu can either be provided with aria-labelledby or aria-label props. Each list item has a presentation role whereas anchor elements have a menuitem role with aria-label referring to the label of the item and aria-disabled defined if the item is disabled. A submenu within a Menu uses the group role with an aria-labelledby defined as the id of the submenu root menuitem label. In popup mode, the component implicitly manages the aria-expanded , aria-haspopup and aria-controls attributes of the target element to define the relation between the target and the popup. Keyboard Support Key Function tab Add focus to the first item if focus moves in to the menu. If the focus is already within the menu, focus moves to the next focusable item in the page tab sequence. shift + tab Add focus to the last item if focus moves in to the menu. If the focus is already within the menu, focus moves to the previous focusable item in the page tab sequence. enter Activates the focused menuitem. If menu is in overlay mode, popup gets closes and focus moves to target. space Activates the focused menuitem. If menu is in overlay mode, popup gets closes and focus moves to target. escape If menu is in overlay mode, popup gets closes and focus moves to target. down arrow Moves focus to the next menuitem. up arrow Moves focus to the previous menuitem. home Moves focus to the first menuitem. end Moves focus to the last menuitem.

sections.label: Basic
sections.description: Menu requires a collection of menuitems as its model .

sections.label: Command
sections.description: The function to invoke when an item is clicked is defined using the command property.

sections.label: Group
sections.description: Menu supports one level of nesting by defining children with items property.

sections.label: Popup
sections.description: Popup mode is enabled by setting popup property to true and calling toggle method with an event of the target.

sections.label: Router
sections.description: Menu items support navigation via routerLink, programmatic routing using commands, or external URLs.

sections.label: Template
sections.description: Menu offers item customization with the item template that receives the menuitem instance from the model as a parameter. The submenu header has its own submenuheader template, additional slots named start and end are provided to embed content before or after the menu.

examples.typescript:
import { Component, OnInit } from '@angular/core'; import { MenuItem } from 'voxx-ui/api'; import { Menu } from 'voxx-ui/menu'; @Component({ template: `
PRIMEAPP {{ item.label }} {{ item.label }} {{ item.shortcut }}
`, standalone: true, imports: [] }) export class MenuTemplateDemo implements OnInit { items: MenuItem[] | undefined; ngOnInit() { this.items = [ { separator: true }, { label: 'Documents', items: [ { label: 'New', icon: 'pi pi-plus', shortcut: '⌘+N' }, { label: 'Search', icon: 'pi pi-search', shortcut: '⌘+S' } ] }, { label: 'Profile', items: [ { label: 'Settings', icon: 'pi pi-cog', shortcut: '⌘+O' }, { label: 'Messages', icon: 'pi pi-inbox', badge: '2' }, { label: 'Logout', icon: 'pi pi-sign-out', shortcut: '⌘+Q', linkClass: '!text-red-500 dark:!text-red-400' } ] }, { separator: true } ]; } }

components.name: message
components.title: Angular Message Component
components.description: Message component is used to display inline messages.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader Message component uses alert role that implicitly defines aria-live as "assertive" and aria-atomic as "true". Since any attribute is passed to the root element, attributes like aria-labelledby and aria-label can optionally be used as well. Close element is a button with an aria-label that refers to the aria.close property of the locale API by default. Close Button Keyboard Support Key Function enter Closes the message. space Closes the message.

sections.label: Basic
sections.description: Message component requires a content to display.

sections.label: Closable
sections.description: Enable closable option to display an icon to remove a message.

sections.label: Dynamic
sections.description: Multiple messages can be displayed using the standard for block.

sections.label: form-doc
sections.description: Validation errors in a form are displayed with the error severity.

sections.label: Icon
sections.description: The icon of a message is specified with the icon property.

components.name: metergroup
components.title: Angular MeterGroup Component
components.description: MeterGroup displays scalar measurements within a known range.

sections.label: Accessibility
sections.description: Screen Reader MeterGroup component uses meter role in addition to the aria-valuemin , aria-valuemax and aria-valuenow attributes. Value to describe the component can be defined using aria-labelledby prop. Keyboard Support Component does not include any interactive elements.

sections.label: Basic
sections.description: MeterGroup requires a value as the data to display where each item in the collection should be a type of MeterItem .

sections.label: Icon
sections.description: Icons can be displayed next to the labels instead of the default marker.

sections.label: Label
sections.description: The position of the labels relative to the meters is defined using the labelPosition property. The default orientation of the labels is horizontal, and the vertical alternative is available through the labelOrientation option.

sections.label: Min Max
sections.description: Boundaries are configured with the min and max values whose defaults are 0 and 100 respectively.

sections.label: Multiple
sections.description: Adding more items to the array displays the meters in a group.

sections.label: Template
sections.description: MeterGroup provides templating support for labels, meter items, and content around the meters.

sections.label: Vertical
sections.description: Layout of the MeterGroup is configured with the orientation property that accepts either horizontal or vertical as available options.
